rationale: Both candidates converge on the same plausible diagnosis (PR #4834 added an intentional wake-after-level-2-sleep guard; the pre-existing test wasn't updated) but both overclaim a confident 'already fixed, close it' resolution that the actual thread doesn't support — the ground-truth comments show ongoing triage, with Flink-ddd explicitly asking to trigger a full CI run to verify, not a confirmed fix. X is cleaner, better organized, and adds genuinely useful practical guidance for users hitting this at runtime, though its very specific line ranges/PR merge date read as risky invented precision. Y has the same core content but its output leaks raw pipeline/task-state scaffolding ('Run report', task dict, duplicated draft_answer plus a second write-up) that is unprofessional and not maintainer-style, hurting completeness.
